name: drivebase
description: Drivebase is a cloud-agnostic file management platform for people and teams using multiple storage providers. 🚀
readme_summary: Drivebase is an open-source, self-hosted platform that unifies multiple cloud storage providers into a single desktop-like interface. It allows users to browse, manage, and transfer files across services like Google Drive, S3, and local storage seamlessly.
key_features: Desktop shell interface in the browser
Support for Google Drive, AWS S3, local filesystem, Dropbox, OneDrive
Batch operations (copy, move, transfer, delete) across providers
primary_use_case: To provide a unified and intuitive interface for managing files across multiple cloud storage services, accessible from a single desktop-like application.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, open desktop computer monitor displaying a vibrant, organized file explorer interface. From the monitor, multiple distinct streams of digital data flow outwards, each transforming into a different cloud storage icon (e.g., Google Drive's G, an S3 bucket, a Dropbox logo). These streams converge into a single, organized pathway leading back into the monitor's file explorer. Subtle glowing lines connect the icons to the central pathway, symbolizing unified management. The overall aesthetic is clean, modern, and slightly futuristic, with a focus on clarity and connectivity. The repository name 'Drivebase' is subtly integrated into the monitor's bezel or as a small desktop icon. 3D isometric illustration style, high detail, vibrant colors.

name: winpodx
readme_summary: WinPodX allows Windows applications to run as native Linux applications, integrating them seamlessly into the Linux desktop environment. It uses FreeRDP RemoteApp and a zero-configuration approach to provide a native experience with real icons and taskbar integration.
key_features: Native Linux windows for Windows apps
Bare-metal disguise for VM detection evasion
primary_use_case: Running Windows applications as if they were native Linux applications on a Linux desktop.
cover_image_prompt: A magical portal opens on a clean, modern Linux desktop, seamlessly blending a Windows application window (like Microsoft Word) into the Linux environment. The Windows app appears as a native window with a familiar Linux icon on the taskbar. On one side of the portal, a user is interacting with the Windows app, while on the other side, the Linux desktop shows the app integrated as if it were a native program. Subtle digital particles flow through the portal, representing the data transfer. The repository name 'WinPodX' is subtly etched into the frame of the portal. The scene is brightly lit with a focus on the seamless integration, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant, clean colors.

name: react-bits
description: An open source collection of animated, interactive & fully customizable React components for building memorable websites.
readme_summary: React Bits is an open-source library offering over 130 free, customizable animated React components for building engaging web interfaces. It focuses on providing ready-to-use text animations, UI elements, and backgrounds, with minimal dependencies and high adaptability.
key_features: Fully customizable via props or source code
Copy-paste ready for modern React projects
Free creative tools: Background Studio, Shape Magic, Texture Lab
primary_use_case: To enable developers to quickly integrate visually appealing and interactive animations and UI components into their React applications without extensive custom development.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, magical workshop where a craftsman is assembling intricate, glowing gears and animated particles. The craftsman is using a blueprint that shows abstract shapes transforming into dynamic text and vibrant background patterns. Small, stylized React logos are subtly integrated into the tools and the workshop's machinery. The overall mood is creative and energetic, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The scene should be rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing the transformation of static elements into lively, animated components, with a bright and inviting color palette.

name: qiaomu-anything-to-notebooklm
description: Claude Skill: Multi-source content processor for NotebookLM. Supports WeChat articles, web pages, YouTube, PDF, Markdown, search queries → Podcast/PPT/MindMap/Quiz etc.
readme_summary: This Python repository acts as a multi-source content processor for NotebookLM, leveraging Claude AI to convert various input formats like web pages, PDFs, and audio into structured outputs such as podcasts, presentations, mind maps, and quizzes. It features advanced capabilities like bypassing paywalls on over 300 websites.
key_features: Multi-source content processing (WeChat articles, web pages, YouTube, PDF, Markdown, search queries, etc.)
Content conversion to various formats (Podcast, PPT, MindMap, Quiz, Video, Report, Infographic, Flashcards)
OCR for scanned PDFs and images
Integration with Claude Code Skill and Google NotebookLM
primary_use_case: To ingest diverse content from various online and offline sources, process it using AI, and transform it into structured, digestible formats suitable for learning and presentation via NotebookLM.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, magical librarian standing at a grand, ornate desk overflowing with diverse input materials: a steaming cup of coffee (representing web pages), a rolled-up ancient scroll (PDF), a glowing YouTube play button, and a stack of newspapers. The librarian is using a shimmering, ethereal quill pen to draw streams of light that transform these inputs into distinct output forms: a miniature podcast microphone emitting sound waves, a small, perfectly formed PowerPoint slide, a intricate 3D mind map, and a quiz board with glowing question marks. The background is a cozy, book-filled study with subtle digital particles and UI elements hinting at AI processing. The overall mood is one of effortless transformation and knowledge synthesis. Style: 3D isometric illustration with vibrant colors and clear iconography.

name: supertonic
description: Lightning-Fast, On-Device, Multilingual TTS — running natively via ONNX.
readme_summary: Supertonic is a high-performance, on-device multilingual text-to-speech (TTS) system that runs natively using ONNX Runtime. It offers fast, real-time speech synthesis across various platforms without cloud dependency, ensuring privacy and minimal overhead.
key_features: Edge-device ready (desktop, mobile, browser, resource-constrained hardware)
Zero network dependency and complete privacy
Expression tags for natural speech nuance
Multi-runtime SDKs (Python, Node.js, WebGPU, Java, C++, C#, Go, Swift, iOS, Rust, Flutter)
primary_use_case: To provide a fast, private, and efficient on-device text-to-speech solution for applications requiring real-time speech synthesis across multiple languages and platforms.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot character sitting at a clean, modern desk, holding a small, glowing microphone. Before the robot is a stack of diverse books and scrolls representing different languages and texts. The robot is speaking, and from its mouth emanates a clear, flowing stream of musical notes and sound waves that form into a beautiful, audible waveform graphic. Subtle UI elements showing language selection and audio output levels are visible on a nearby screen. The overall mood is efficient and creative, with a clean, bright aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with smooth gradients and clear, friendly character design, emphasizing the transformation of text into natural-sounding speech locally.
